name: Fintech (US)
description: Curated framework bundle for fintech businesses operating in the United States — payment processors, neobanks, lenders, brokerage front-ends and money-services businesses.

crosswalk_pairs.rationale: ISO 27001 Annex A.5 / A.8 controls evidence the SOC 2 Common Criteria — operate one ISMS and produce both reports.
PCI DSS Req. 3 / 4 / 7 / 8 / 10 maps onto ISO 27001 A.8 cryptography + A.5 access control — the same control evidence supports both.
CSF 2.0 GV / ID / PR / DE / RS / RC functions map cleanly to ISO 27001 Clause 4.1 and Annex A.5 / A.8 — used by federal counterparties as their common reference.

sequencing_notes: 1. PCI DSS scope-reduction first — tokenise to a PSP wherever possible; the residual SAQ shape determines how much of Req. 3 / 4 / 10 you actually own.
2. Stand up SOC 2 Type I (point-in-time) within the first 6 months; convert to Type II (12-month observation) once controls are operating.
3. Use NIST CSF 2.0 as the organising taxonomy — it is the lingua franca with US bank counterparties and federal agencies and reads onto ISO 27001 if you ever add it.
4. CCPA / CPRA applies if you have California consumers; the right-to-know, right-to-delete and opt-out-of-sale flows must be wired before user signup goes live in CA.
5. NIST AI RMF 1.0 only if you score / decision with ML (credit, fraud, underwriting) — its GOVERN / MAP / MEASURE / MANAGE functions slot under SOC 2 CC1 governance.
6. HIPAA only if you are a covered entity or a business associate handling PHI (e.g. health-savings-account products with an insurer).
